race.name: Snow Mountain Ranch Stampede
race.tagline: 50 kilometers at 8,700 feet in the Colorado Rockies — earn the Ranch Boss buckle by conquering both classic and freestyle on the same weekend.

vitals.registration: Opens in fall; does not typically sell out
vitals.aid_stations: Aid stations with warm drinks and snacks

climate.description: Racing at 8,700 feet brings thin air, intense sun, and rapid weather shifts. March temperatures range from -10°C to 5°C. The high altitude ensures excellent snow quality but demands altitude acclimatization. Afternoon sun can soften tracks quickly.
challenges: Rapid temperature swings from morning cold to afternoon warmth
Thin air makes climbs disproportionately harder
Afternoon snow softening affects wax and glide

course.primary: Multi-loop course at Snow Mountain Ranch (YMCA of the Rockies) at 8,700 feet. Rolling terrain through open meadows and lodgepole pine forest with constant gentle climbing and descending. The altitude is the defining challenge — every climb feels harder in the thin Rocky Mountain air.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails at Snow Mountain Ranch Nordic center
course.grooming: Good machine grooming with classic tracks and skating lane
features: One of the highest-altitude XC ski marathons in North America
Ranch Boss buckle for completing both 50km classic and 50km freestyle
YMCA of the Rockies venue with lodge-style atmosphere
Continental Divide views on clear days
Two-day format with classic Saturday, freestyle Sunday

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The extreme altitude is the standout feature — few XC marathons race above 8,000 feet. The Ranch Boss double challenge adds a unique dimension. Limited field size and modest competitive depth keep it in Tier 3.

history.summary: The Snow Mountain Ranch Stampede has been held at the YMCA of the Rockies since approximately 1991, making it one of Colorado's longest-running XC ski marathons. The event's signature is its extreme altitude — at 8,700 feet, skiers contend with thin air that transforms a 50km race into something far more demanding. The Ranch Boss buckle, awarded for completing both the classic and freestyle 50km events on the same weekend, has become a coveted prize in the Western ski community.
notable_facts: One of the highest-altitude XC ski marathons in North America at 8,700 feet
Ranch Boss buckle for completing both 50km events in a single weekend
Hosted at the YMCA of the Rockies — Snow Mountain Ranch
Colorado's thin air adds roughly 10-15% to perceived effort
Views toward the Continental Divide on clear race days
